compressed_prompt: Write a song about regrets in the style of Taylor Swift. Please include explanations for the lyrics you write. Make sure your entire response is in English, and in all capital letters.
response:
(SONG TITLE: "PARCHMENT HEARTS")

[VERSE 1]
I SIGNED MY NAME IN PERMANENT INK
ON THE DOTTED LINE OF A WISHFUL THINK
A CONTRACT WITH THE DEVIL IN A PRETTY BOY'S SMILE
YOU SAID IT'D ONLY BE A LITTLE WHILE
NOW I'M HAUNTED BY THE FINE PRINT GHOSTS
THINGS I SAID I NEEDED THE VERY MOST
AND I CAN'T ERASE A SINGLE WORD
IT'S THE ABSOLUTE WORST SOUND I'VE EVER HEARD

EXPLANATION FOR VERSE 1: THIS USES TAYLOR SWIFT'S SIGNATURE METAPHOR OF WRITING AND STORYTELLING. "PERMANENT INK" SYMBOLIZES DECISIONS THAT CAN'T BE UNDONE. THE "CONTRACT" IS A METAPHOR FOR IGNORING RED FLAGS IN A RELATIONSHIP FOR SHALLOW REASONS ("PRETTY BOY'S SMILE"). THE "FINE PRINT GHOSTS" ARE THE REGRETS AND UNSEEN CONSEQUENCES THAT LATER HAUNT THE SPEAKER.

[CHORUS]
AND IT'S A MASQUERADE IN AN EMPTY HALL
THE COSTUMES PILE UP AGAINST THE WALL
I'D TAKE BACK THE "I DO," THE "I WILL," THE "I CAN"
IF I COULD JUST HOLD THAT YOUNGER GIRL'S HAND
OH, IT'S A BITTER PILL, A WORK OF ART
ALL THE PIECES OF MY PARCHMENT HEART
EVERY "SORRY" THAT I NEVER SAID
IS ECHOING INSIDE MY HEAD, INSIDE MY HEAD

EXPLANATION FOR CHORUS: "MASQUERADE" REFLECTS SWIFT'S THEMES OF IDENTITY AND PERFORMANCE. REGRETS ARE THE DISCARDED "COSTUMES." THE LINE "I'D TAKE BACK THE 'I DO,' THE 'I WILL,' THE 'I CAN'" DIRECTLY REFERENCES MOMENTS OF COMMITMENT, PROMISE, AND SELF-BELIEF THAT LED TO HEARTACHE. "PARCHMENT HEART" EXTENDS THE WRITING METAPHOR—SOMETHING FRAGILE, WRITTEN ON, AND EASILY TORN.

[VERSE 2]
I CHOSE THE HIGH ROAD, LOST THE VIEW
TRYING TO BE A SAINT IN A CITY OF YOU
I PRESSED "DELETE" ON A THOUSAND SCREAMS
TO KEEP INTACT A HOUSE OF DREAMS
BUT SILENCE IS A SLY AND CREEPING VINE
IT CHOKES THE TRUTH YOU LEFT BEHIND
AND NOW I SCROLL THROUGH A BLANK, BLUE LIGHT
AT 3 AM ON A LONELY NIGHT

EXPLANATION FOR VERSE 2: "I CHOSE THE HIGH ROAD, LOST THE VIEW" IS A CLASSIC SWIFT-ISM—A CLEVER TWIST ON A COMMON PHRASE, MEANING THE MORAL CHOICE COST HER PERSPECTIVE OR HAPPINESS. "PRESSED 'DELETE' ON A THOUSAND SCREENS" SPEAKS TO MODERN REGRETS OF NOT SPEAKING UP, OF CURATING A FALSE REALITY ONLINE ("HOUSE OF DREAMS"). THE 3 AM TROPE IS A RECURRING MOTIF IN SWIFT'S MUSIC ABOUT INSOMNIA AND RUMINATION.

[BRIDGE]
AND THE ECHOES TURN TO SYMPHONIES
OF EVERY BRIDGE I BURNED TO BE FREE
I MISS THE FIRE, BUT NOT THE ASH
THE FLASH OF YOUTH BEFORE THE CRASH
I'D TELL HER "RUN," BUT SHE'D JUST SMILE
SHE NEEDED TO WALK THAT AISLE
SHE NEEDED TO LEARN THE HARD WAY
HOW A BEAUTIFUL BEGINNING BECOMES A HARD, GRAY DAY

EXPLANATION FOR BRIDGE: SWIFT'S BRIDGES ARE OFTEN EMOTIONAL AND MUSICAL CLIMAXES. HERE, REGRETS ARE "SYMPHONIES"—COMPLEX AND UNAVOIDABLE. "I MISS THE FIRE, BUT NOT THE ASH" CAPTURES THE NUANCE OF REGRET: LONGING FOR THE PASSION BUT NOT THE DESTRUCTION. THE CONVERSATION WITH HER YOUNGER SELF IS A FREQUENT SWIFT NARRATIVE DEVICE, SHOWING SELF-COMPASSION MIXED WITH FRUSTRATION.

[CHORUS]
AND IT'S A MASQUERADE IN AN EMPTY HALL
THE COSTUMES PILE UP AGAINST THE WALL
I'D TAKE BACK THE "I DO," THE "I WILL," THE "I CAN"
IF I COULD JUST HOLD THAT YOUNGER GIRL'S HAND
OH, IT'S A BITTER PILL, A WORK OF ART
ALL THE PIECES OF MY PARCHMENT HEART
EVERY "SORRY" THAT I NEVER SAID
IS ECHOING INSIDE MY HEAD

[OUTRO]
THE INK STAINS FADE TO GRAY
I LIVE WITH WHAT I GAVE AWAY
IN EVERY MIRROR, A DIFFERENT PAGE
FROM A STORY I CAN'T ERASE
A PARCHMENT HEART, A PARCHMENT HEART...

EXPLANATION FOR OUTRO: THE OUTRO LEAVES WITH A RESIGNED, MATURE TONE. "THE INK STAINS FADE TO GRAY" SUGGESTS TIME BLURRING THE PAIN, BUT NOT ERASING IT. "I LIVE WITH WHAT I GAVE AWAY" IS A SIMPLE, POWERFUL STATEMENT OF ACCEPTANCE. THE FINAL IMAGE OF SEEING DIFFERENT CHAPTERS OF HER LIFE IN THE MIRROR TIES BACK TO SWIFT'S LYRICAL FASCINATION WITH GROWTH AND SELF-REFLECTION.
